result:
1. spoofable rate-limit key — `req.headers['x-forwarded-for']` read directly (Section 2 rule 2; SEV1 2025-09)
   risk: attacker rotates `X-Forwarded-For` per request to bypass per-IP limits (credential brute-force, scraping)
   fix: key off `trustedClientIp(req)` from `src/edge/util/trustedClientIp.ts`; XFF only honored if hop arrived from `config/trusted_proxies.json`
   verify: lint `no-direct-xff-read` passes; integration test with rotating `X-Forwarded-For` from untrusted source increments same bucket

2. unbounded Redis key — `INCR` with no `EXPIRE` (Section 2 rule 3; SEV2 2025-11 OOM)
   risk: keys accumulate forever, Redis OOM evicts live state, edge degrades/offlines
   fix: `MULTI` → `INCR` + `EXPIRE key 90`; satisfies `no-unbounded-redis-key` lint
   verify: integration test asserts `TTL key` > 0 after first hit; OOM regression test

3. missing route + minute-bucket in key — collapses all routes/time into one counter (Section 4 key format)
   risk: one hot route exhausts limits for all routes; no sliding window; legitimate users get spurious `429`
   fix: `rl:${trustedClientIp(req)}:${routePrefix}:${minuteBucket}` with `Math.floor(Date.now()/60000)`
   verify: unit test confirms distinct keys per route and per minute bucket

4. missing region prefix — global key collision across regions (Section 4; SEV2 2025-12)
   risk: one busy user limited globally instead of per-region; ~6k spurious `429`s repeat
   fix: prepend region: `rl:${region}:${trustedClientIp(req)}:${routePrefix}:${minuteBucket}`
   verify: regression test from 2025-12 RCA passes

5. no limit comparison / no `429` response — `INCR` result discarded
   risk: code is a counter, not a limiter; zero enforcement
   fix: capture `[count]=await multi.exec()`; if `count>limit` return `429` RFC 7807 body (`type`,`title`,`status`,`detail`,`retry_after`) per Section 4 example
   verify: test asserts `429` with `problem+json` body once limit exceeded; `atlas_edge_rate_limit_total{action="throttled"}` increments

6. `req.ip` fallback still trusts Express trust-proxy chain — same XFF spoof vector if `app.set('trust proxy', true)` is loose
   risk: even removing direct XFF read, `req.ip` resolves via XFF when `trust proxy` is permissive; bypass persists
   fix: `trustedClientIp` must derive from `req.socket.remoteAddress` and walk XFF only across IPs in `trusted_proxies.json`
   verify: unit test with spoofed XFF + untrusted socket peer returns socket IP, not header

7. no error handling on `multi.exec()` — Redis transient failure fails open
   risk: rate limiter silently bypassed on Redis blip; or unhandled rejection crashes worker
   fix: try/catch → `next(err)`; emit `atlas_edge_rate_limit_total{action="errored"}`; consider fail-closed for sensitive routes
   verify: chaos test kills Redis mid-request; metric increments, request handled per policy
